one_line_ask: Grade and consistently crop twelve raw bakery product photos, vectorize the Crumb & Co. wordmark, upload the prepped assets, then run the client's authored InDesign catalog template through a data-merge against the wholesale roster CSV to produce a print-ready B2B PDF and a high-resolution JPEG.
brief: Crumb & Co., a small-batch wholesale bakery, needs its B2B distributor catalog finished for print and PDF hand-out. The owner has already authored an InDesign catalog template (crumbco_catalog_template.indd) — an 8.5x11 portrait, multi-page grid layout with repeating product cells, each cell holding an image frame plus real InDesign data-merge fields (<<product_name>>, <<sku>>, <<description>>, <<case_pack>>, <<wholesale_price>>) and section headers for Breads, Pastries, Cakes & Slices, and Cookies & Bars, with a masthead frame for the wordmark and a full-bleed cover panel. The owner has also handed off the wholesale product roster as a CSV (12 SKUs across the four sections, each with a keyword-rich product description, case-pack quantity, and B2B wholesale price) and twelve raw product photos shot quickly on the bakery counter — sourdough boules, seeded rye, croissants, cinnamon rolls, a layer cake, brownies, chocolate-chip cookies, and more. The photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, flat and dull, cool fluorescent white balance, and cluttered counter backgrounds.
